Important: Please Save Your Own Copy Before Editing :) 



SEVEN FIGURE EMAILS #6 

Couple’s Therapy



Ry Schwartz & Copyhackers

2020



SEVEN FIGURE EMAILS #6

About The “Couple’s Therapy”

The Persuasion Deep Dive

Where to send it:

Message Mining Q’s:

Themeplate

See it in action:

Example: Copy Hackers - Copy  School 17

Example 2 - DCA 2019 Non-Reg #1

About The “Couple’s Therapy” 

The Couple’s Therapy themeplate is an advanced version of the classic “Perfect for you if” template that was introduced in 10x Emails and 10x Launches. 



It’s a form of the PFYI -- but with a lead that seeks to overcome inertia/complacency, trigger the consistency principle, and remind your audience that NOW is the time act.  



At its core, it’s about reminding your audience of their initial core intent in signing up to your email list, and encouraging consistency with that initial intent.



This is especially important if there’s been a significant lag between their original sign up -- and the offer you’re putting in front of them… OR if they’ve been receiving a ton of personality-based content (or content that sways away from the initial intent.)   



When we create content and leverage our personalities in our brand, sometimes our audience forgets the core implicit agreement in our relationship.



But for any relationship (or sale) to occur, there needs to be a clear, recognizable value exchange. 



If that value has been obscured (either by time or content that doesn’t always enforce the main value exchange and your authority to deliver it), it’s time to remind  your audience of the core implicit agreement that drives your relationship forward. 

The Persuasion Deep Dive



Implied Commitment & Consistency: If you’re emailing them, it means that “once upon a time” they signed up to your list to either overcome a problem or achieve a result. We need our prospect to reconnect with that initial intent / core implicit agreement… and stay consistent with it* 



Re-Establishing Authority: If your content or messaging has been broad or personality-based, you’ve given VALUE, built trust, and maybe even bonded with your audience -- but you’ve also diluted your authority around what your offer solves and maybe even coached complacency. This template is about re-densifying your authority around your offer. 



Identifying as a Buyer (via the PFYI section): Just like how your prospect needs to re-relate to you as an authority on the topic -- they need to remember who THEY are within the context of your relationship. Who are they on the “customer” side of the value exchange? We use the “PFYI” format to help mirror back the qualities/characteristics of a perfect buyer. 



* writing tip: this isn’t a time to be timid. You need to write with the presumption that anyone on your list receiving this email signed up with the INTENT to solve the problem that your product solves. Naturally, this won’t be true for a portion of your list, but in writing this email, you’ll either awaken that intent OR filter out non-buyers. 

Where to send it:

As an alternative to the PFYI themeplate (from 10x Launches / 10x Emails)

As a standalone sales email

As an opening day launch email to non-registrants (or non-active subscribers) 

Message Mining Q’s:



For Intro and Lead:



What is the CORE intent / commitment that my perfect customer would’ve had upon signing up for my list? What did they want to achieve and/or avoid? (MOHT/MOHT) 



Why did they believe that I could uniquely help them with it? (what might’ve been my perceived USP) 



For PFYI Section:



How are they already investing time/energy/money in solving the problem/achieving the outcome?



What’s your product’s ultimate outcome?



What’s your product’s quickest, most valuable win?



What’s your customer’s minimum viable commitment to achieve it? 

Themeplate

[Relational Recap] 

Core Intent 1: MOHP/T]

Core Intent 2: MOHP/T]

Core Intent 3: MOHP/T] 

[Close Commitment Loop with Product] 

[Transition to PFYI section, either by]

Presuming that based on R1-R3,  they SHOULD have said “yes”, but there’s fear/hesitance still present] - see CS 2017 Example. Or… 

THIS has been the moment they’ve been waiting for to make good on that commitment -- and back that up through your USP. - see DCA19 Example

[PFYI Section Options] - 

The person just getting started who wants to do it right the first time

The person already “in it”, struggling, and who wants to finally fix it once and for all

Alternative Avatar

General desirable trait (ie. willing to put in the work)

Specific trait/actions to back it up (ie. investing 4 hrs/week trying to solve it) 

Aware and excited of big final outcome

Aware and excited about the quickest, most valuable win

Willing to take the minimum viable commitment / take you up on guarantee

Aware of bonuses / discounts / perks of buying now as opposed to later 

[Final CTA]

[Optional PS: Testimonials/Case study]



See it in action: 

Example: Copy Hackers - Copy School 17 



Subject:

re: our relationship 



Body:



Short History Lesson:

At some point our paths crossed.

You said:

Hey Jo, teach me how to do this email copy thing.

Plz, plz, plzzzz show me...

How to write the kinds of emails that make people get addicted to our brand and recommend us like crazy



How to string together email sequences and launch campaigns that crush those lofty goals I set with my team on that last Zoom meeting



And behind it all, you said:

"Show me how to stop guessing at every word while generating big lifts, hitting hockey-stick-forming acquisition goals and getting killer results in less time because I'm now a CTCM (Confident and Talented Conversion Mofo)"



Then a few days ago...

I hopped onto my ConvertKit soapbox, and said...

Here you go :-)

And while your inner growth marketer was screaming "hell yes, sign me up""...

Your fearful, procrastinating lizard brain (aka the one that points out job listings for middle management positions at Oracle) said, "Whooaaa. Slow down there, champ. Let's wait a sec and see how we feel later."

Well now's later.

Your lizard brain's trying to hijack the controls.

And unless you and I partner up to do something about it right here, right now...

Your next big project (if you even have the confidence to get it out the door) will be straight-jacketed and sabotaged by the part of your brain still trying to avoid being Vitamix'd by an imaginary T-rex.

Not cool.

So here's how we're going to soothe that bobble-eyed desert-dweller.

Your lizard brain doesn't care if Copy School is "right for you."

"right" doesn't help you sleep at night.

"right" doesn't generate the cash to cover payroll with a single email send.

"right" doesn't pack qualified leads onto your list - and then satisfy them with the copy that shows them your product was *made* for them.

I don't want "right."

You don't want "right."

And your lizard brain definitely doesn't want "right."

What it really wants is to know you're safe.

Better than safe.

Big-payoff, zero-risk kinda safe.

So if you and your lizard brain can check off 5 of the 7 boxes below, I invite you to slip that lizard a stiff drink while you and I conspire to make you part of the top 1% of growth marketers on the planet.

Coolio?

Here are the 7 Ways to Know You're Ready to 10x Your ROI using Copy School:

1. You're already doing this copy thing:

Maybe it's for a boss. Maybe it's for a client.

Or maybe you're that rare breed of founder who recognizes how critical it is that you write your own copy... at least part of the time.

Whatever the case, you're intimately aware of the fact that doing it better + faster isn't going to just make your brand "sound good." You don't need a course for that; you need an hour and a touch of creativity. The copywriting you need is the kind that means wider funnels coated in a Griswald-esque non-nutritive cereal varnish - payments go woosh into Stripe! - and all things that allow you to do the stuff growing businesses do.

Like upgrade your software, bring on new team members to whip through that roadmap, buy non-refurbished hardware (fancy!) and finally start taking nights off.

In short...

10x-ing your skills can absolutely mean 10x-ing your profit potential.

And you are...

SO READY FOR THAT

2. You work for an agency or company that sells CRO to clients:

Meaning the skills you pick up in Copy School will essentially make you the most valuable person in your office - the one who takes that word-shaped air that's been masquerading as "copy" and fills it with head-nodding, forward-moving, yes-saying sales copy.

Which, if you don't minimize the insane value you're adding, could earn you some serious job security and negotiating power.

Or if you're like "Jo, I might be over the job-security thing"...

Then you want the skills, templates and confidence to moonlight a few extra hours per week as a highly paid freelancer... so you can venture off on your own.

In either case, you're definitely upgrading from Keurig to Nespresso and walking around with Jagger-esque swagger that makes the whole joint take notice. 

3. You're an online entrepreneur, course creator, publisher, coach or consultant with a product to sell and people to sell it to:

Aka the "no brainer special" part 2. 

If you're already driving paid traffic to a landing page, what would it mean if you could capture, say, two times the leads using the strategies from the first two modules of 10x Landing Pages alone?

If you're already sending emails to your list, what would it mean if you could actually plan your drip campaign and write emails that funnel more qualified leads into your conversion-ready landing pages?

Or if you took just one of the 19 email templates inside 10x Launches and cued it up in your evergreen funnel?

At the very least, you're in the BEST possible position to sign up for Copy School

...and to know in the first 10 videos if it's going to be one the best investments you've made in your biz to date. Because you've tried guessing at this stuff. And, well, if the guesswork was working, you wouldn't be reading this, would you?

4. You're already investing in adding conversion copywriting to your marketing stack:

You've read the blog posts.

Watched the webinars.

And stuck your nose in more than one copywriting classic.

Gene Schwartz, Claude Hopkins and John Caples are your homeboys.

They put your days of Ogilvy praise to shame.

You may not understand everything they have to say, but damn do you sound smart when you name-drop them on a marketing team call.

Chances are you've invested cash.

At the very least, you've invested time.

And either way...

The prospect of having a complete A-Z training that covers EVERYTHING you need to optimize those funnels your biz keeps pouring cash into is *pinch me now* good.

5. Honestly, you value your time scrolling through your Facebook newsfeed

So here's the thing.

In Copy School, you're getting 3 of our BEST copywriting trainings.

It's like a degree in conversion copywriting.

But that doesn't mean you'll have to invest 10 hours a week for the next 4 months to start seeing results. Not at all.

Copy School is engineered for immediate ROI.

You don't have to be a copywriter (or even want to be one) to join Copy School.

Of course, once you're halfway through Copy School, you'll have more copywriting know-how than 95% of the world's copywriters. But we'll only call you a copywriter if you want us to.

The plan is this: get you real, tangible results "right out of the box" - and then you go ahead and use that leverage and confidence to grow that thing you're trying to grow. You know. That thing. "That business thing" your Auntie Carol hopes is a passing phase - but that is the reason you wake up early, stay up late, geek out on growth posts and actually want to know how to write copy that converts.

But we also understand you're hella busy.

So I don't want you to swap out weekend-at-the-cottage time in order to go through Copy School.

Instead, replace 2 hours of mindless FB newsfeed-scrolling per week with time spent applying the lessons, templates, strategies and processes that will have the greatest (and fastest) impact on your business.

Yes, I'm ready to modify my fb addiction with a healthier, more profitable conversion addiction but I understand that Jo would never expect me to fully give up quality time watching cat videos and taking mental breaks

6. You feel at ease knowing you have a FULL 60 Days to give it a risk-free shot:

Maybe you won't be able to dive in for a few weeks.

Maybe you're still new to this copy thing, and need some extra time to apply what you're learning.

Either way, you totally appreciate having 2 FULL MONTHS to give Copy School an honest shot

7. You're in business - and all that that entails:

Most startups need cashflow.

Which is why your eyes scan the page to find the way to join Copy School without a large upfront investment.

You're looking for a manageable monthly payment - because you are more than certain your landing pages could grab more people and your sales emails could convert more people, but wouldn't it be grand to divide the investment in your new skills into these nice little manageable bits?

You can - choose the monthly plan here

Or maybe you've just had a decent bump in the bank account...

That big client finally paid their outstanding invoice. Or you did a big push and it worked (and you know it could work better with the right copy training - ahem).

So you're ready to pay in full - but when you do that, you want a reward of some kind. Like, how about exclusive access to a private training session where we'll break down EXACTLY how we planned and wrote copy for the launch of Copy School? (Meta!)

Join here and get this bonus when you choose the one-time payment

Whichever payment option feels best for you, please do choose before 4pm PDT tomorrow. That's when Copy School enrolment ends.

~jo

PS: About 6 months ago, our friend Tarzan Kay took just 2 of the 3 programs inside Copy School - and embarked on her FIRST affiliate launch.

Here's what happened:

“Just wrapped up my very first campaign for a super competitive affiliate launch. Using the strategies and templates from both 10x Emails and 10x Landing Pages, I was able to hit over $15K in revenue! These programs are making money for me over and over again” - Tarzan Kay

She's since joined 10x Launches, too.

You'll get everything she got when you join Copy School.

It's the same stuff that draws the likes of Google and IBM to request our help - and that makes incredible folks like Todd Herman say:

“Joanna is the New England Patriots of copy and email strategy. Everything is just championship calibre.”

If you want the very stuff that's helped hundreds of digital businesses and service-based businesses whip their words into shape, give up those days of guessing and... 

Meet me over here so I can hook you up with your login deets right away



Example 2 - DCA 2019 Non-Reg #1



Subject:

Now enrolling: Digital Course Academy

NOW OPEN: Digital Course Academy



Body:



Short history lesson: 



At some points our paths crossed.



You said:



Hey Amy, please show me everything you know about creating and launching digital courses.



Please show me… 



How to outline, organize and record my lessons in the most effective way possible so that i can finally get my expertise packaged into a beautiful digital course instead of “talking about it” for another year. 



Show me how to actually launch my program with impact so that I can feel like my efforts had a massive payoff. 



And behind it all, you said… 



Teach me the advanced marketing strategies that allow me to make my course the CORNERSTONE of my online business, pulling in profits in a time-leveraged way so that i can free myself from the time for money trap and ONLY take on projects and clients that I absolutely love.



And to that I say…



Hold my bulletproof coffee ;-) 



In case you missed it, we just launched our brand new implementation program, Digital Course Academy:



Graphic: Now Enrolling -- Digital Course Academy 



This program has literally been YEARS in the making.



It’s been meticulously engineered from the ground up, giving you EVERYTHING you need to successfully build and launch a digital course business this year. 



If you’ve been hanging out in our community for a while waiting for the right time to finally go “all-in” on building and launching your course…



This is it. 



But I will offer this one important warning.



The goal of Digital Course Academy isn’t to create a fun little course that acts as a glorified calling card or that you offer to clients who can’t afford to work with you 1-on-1.



This is all about creating and launching a massively profitable digital course that can be the primary revenue driver of your business for years to come. 



This is about reshaping your business to be more time-leveraged, automated, and personally free’ing than it’s ever been before.



Therefore, I ONLY recommend that you consider a spot inside Digital Course Academy if you can check off at least 4 of the following: 



You’re already “sold” on creating digital courses. You just haven’t found the right guidance, implementation plans, or accountability to confidently “green light” the project. 



You’ve already been investing time, energy and money in building your digital course but have suffered from more momentum-crushing missteps and misfires than any entrepreneur can reasonably endure. 



You’re open to gradually restructuring your revenue model to be more driven from digital course sales than 1-on-1 client work or a day job.



You’re excited to start investing the upfront work to create the profitable long-term assets that will provide passive revenue streams for months and years to come.



You’re ready to go “all in” and do the work as long as you feel that every action you take is perfectly-engineered for success. 



You’re excited to build and launch your course in a powerful LIVE format with hundreds of your peers cheering you on (and providing feedback) every step of the way. 



You want to lock in our best introductory rate on Digital Course Academy, along with exclusive bonuses that no one outside of this initial group will ever know existed ;-) 



If you found yourself nodding your head to at least 4 of those criteria…

.

I’d love to personally invite you to check out more details here.



Contrary to popular belief, building and launching a digital course isn’t just a “bolt on” to your existing business.



Done right -- it is your business.



And after building a multi 7-figure business SOLELY off of digital courses (and with just a small team)...



I couldn’t be more excited to lead you step-by-step in creating a more profitable and time-leveraged business in 2019. 



Hope to see you on the other side,



Amy
